skill.name: wiki-architect
skill.description: Analyzes code repositories and generates hierarchical documentation structures with onboarding guides. Use when the user wants to create a wiki, generate documentation, map a codebase structure, or create learning paths for developers.
skill.summary: Generates structured wiki documentation and onboarding guides from codebases
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 13 potential issues, all confirmed as false positives after review. External command patterns were Markdown code spans using backticks for inline code formatting. Cryptographic and reconnaissance patterns were word matches in documentation text. No actual security risks found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Spans Misidentified
low_findings.description: Static scanner detected backtick patterns at SKILL.md:28 and SKILL.md:62 as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are Markdown inline code spans formatting text like 'file_path:line_number' and 'items[].children[]', not code execution.

content.user_title: Generate Wiki Documentation from Any Codebase
content.value_statement: Transform complex codebases into organized, hierarchical documentation with structured learning paths. Automate wiki creation with architecture diagrams, onboarding guides, and developer documentation.
actual_capabilities: Analyze repository structure and identify architectural patterns
Generate hierarchical JSON catalogues with nested documentation sections
Create principal-level guides with architecture diagrams and tradeoffs
Build zero-to-hero learning paths for new developers
Produce getting started guides with setup and usage instructions
Cite specific files and line numbers in all documentation prompts
limitations: Cannot execute code or test runtime behavior during analysis
Limited to static analysis of file structure and content
Requires existing codebase structure to generate meaningful documentation
May need human review for complex domain-specific terminology
Small repositories (10 files or fewer) use simplified structure

use_cases.title: New Team Member Onboarding
use_cases.description: Create comprehensive onboarding guides that help new developers understand codebase architecture, key components, and contribution workflows from day one.

use_cases.title: Legacy System Documentation
use_cases.description: Generate structured documentation for existing projects lacking proper docs, mapping architectural layers and component relationships automatically.

use_cases.title: Open Source Wiki Creation
use_cases.description: Produce professional wikis and documentation sites for open source projects to improve contributor experience and user adoption.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Wiki Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a wiki for this repository
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick start request for basic documentation structure

prompt_templates.title: Onboarding Guide Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an onboarding guide for this codebase that helps new developers understand the architecture and start contributing
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating learning paths for new team members

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this repository and produce a hierarchical documentation catalogue with architecture diagrams, component breakdowns, and file references
prompt_templates.scenario: Deep technical documentation with architectural insights

prompt_templates.title: Complete Wiki with Learning Paths
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a comprehensive wiki including principal-level architecture guide, zero-to-hero learning path, getting started documentation, and deep dive into subsystems with specific file references
prompt_templates.scenario: Full documentation suite for complex projects

output_examples.input: Create a wiki for my Express.js API project
output_examples.output: Generates a JSON catalogue with Onboarding section (principal guide with Mermaid diagrams, zero-to-hero path), Getting Started (setup, usage, quick reference), and Deep Dive sections (presentation layer, business logic, data access, infrastructure) with specific file citations like src/routes/index.js:15 for each component.

output_examples.input: Generate an onboarding guide for this React application
output_examples.output: Creates structured learning path with Part I (React foundations with JavaScript comparisons), Part II (codebase architecture and state management patterns), Part III (dev setup, testing, navigation), plus appendices with 40+ term glossary and key file reference.

best_practices: Provide clear project context when invoking the skill, such as project type and primary goals
Review generated documentation for accuracy and add domain-specific knowledge
Use the output as a template and customize based on team standards and conventions
Keep the generated wiki updated as the codebase evolves
anti_patterns: Expecting runtime behavior analysis without executing the codebase
Using this skill for non-code projects or pure content repositories
Assuming generated documentation replaces human-written explanations entirely
Invoking the skill on empty or minimally populated repositories

faq.question: What types of projects can Wiki Architect analyze?
faq.answer: Wiki Architect works with any codebase including web applications, APIs, libraries, and tools. It detects languages and frameworks from file extensions and build configuration to generate appropriate documentation structures.

faq.question: How does Wiki Architect handle small versus large repositories?
faq.answer: Small repositories with 10 or fewer files receive simplified documentation with Getting Started and Onboarding sections only. Larger repositories get full hierarchical catalogues with Deep Dive sections covering all architectural layers.

faq.question: Can I customize the generated documentation structure?
faq.answer: Yes. The output is a JSON catalogue that serves as a template. You can modify the structure, add sections, adjust prompts, and refine content to match your team's documentation standards and project requirements.

faq.question: Does Wiki Architect execute my code to understand behavior?
faq.answer: No. Wiki Architect performs static analysis only, examining file structure, names, and content. It does not execute code or analyze runtime behavior, keeping the analysis safe and fast.

faq.question: What file formats and programming languages are supported?
faq.answer: The skill detects languages from file extensions and works with any programming language. It automatically selects appropriate comparison languages for cross-learning explanations based on the detected primary language.

faq.question: How accurate are the file references in generated documentation?
faq.answer: File references are based on actual repository structure at the time of analysis. As your codebase changes, regenerate documentation to keep citations current. Always verify references match your current codebase state.
